title: Cytotechnologists
description: Stain, mount, and study cells to detect evidence of cancer, hormonal abnormalities, and other pathological conditions following established standards and practices.

tasks.text: Examine cell samples to detect abnormalities in the color, shape, or size of cellular components and patterns.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Document specimens by verifying patients' and specimens' information.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Submit slides with abnormal cell structures to pathologists for further examination.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare and analyze samples, such as Papanicolaou (PAP) smear body fluids and fine needle aspirations (FNAs), to detect abnormal conditions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Examine specimens, using microscopes, to evaluate specimen quality.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Maintain effective laboratory operations by adhering to standards of specimen collection, preparation, or laboratory safety.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide patient clinical data or microscopic findings to assist pathologists in the preparation of pathology reports.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assist pathologists or other physicians to collect cell samples by fine needle aspiration (FNA) biopsy or other method.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare cell samples by applying special staining techniques, such as chromosomal staining, to differentiate cells or cell components.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Adjust, maintain, or repair laboratory equipment, such as microscopes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assign tasks or coordinate task assignments to ensure adequate performance of laboratory activities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Attend continuing education programs that address laboratory issues.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine specimens to detect abnormal hormone conditions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
